data.title: WhatConverts: Track Real Leads from Marketing Campaigns
data.excerpt: Discover WhatConverts, the marketing attribution platform that reveals which campaigns generate real leads via calls, forms, and chats. See detailed reports and optimize your strategy. Try it now.

$media.alt: WhatConverts: Track Real Leads from Marketing Campaigns

children.text: Disclosure:
This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. We only recommend tools we believe in.

I've spent years in digital marketing, and the single biggest headache has always been the same: knowing what actually works. You pour budget into ads, SEO, content, and social, but when a lead comes in, where did it really come from? Was it that Facebook campaign, the Google Ad, or the blog post they read three months ago? Guessing is expensive. That's why I got so excited when I found
WhatConverts
. It doesn't just track visits; it tracks *conversions*—the real leads that matter to your business. If you're tired of flying blind, this tool is your new best friend.
You can start a free trial here to see for yourself
What is WhatConverts? More Than Just Another Analytics Tool

At its core, WhatConverts is a marketing attribution and lead tracking platform. But that description feels too clinical for what it does. Think of it as a detective for your marketing efforts. It gathers clues—every call, form submission, and live chat from your website—and connects them back to the specific marketing source that triggered them. Instead of vague reports on "website traffic," you get a crystal-clear answer: "This lead came from your Google Ads campaign, keyword 'affordable CRM,' and here's the full call recording."

The platform centralizes everything. You get one dashboard where you can see all your leads, filter by source, medium, campaign, or even keyword. You can listen to call recordings, read chat transcripts, and see the exact form data. This isn't just reporting; it's actionable intelligence. It closes the loop between marketing spend and revenue, which is the holy grail for any business owner or marketer. The setup is surprisingly straightforward, and their support team is incredibly responsive, which is a huge plus in my book.
If you want a step-by-step guide on getting started, check out our tutorial.
Core Features That Make WhatConverts Shine
WhatConverts packs a punch with features designed to give you complete clarity. Let's break down the ones that have made the biggest impact for me.
Multi-Channel Lead Tracking
This is its bread and butter. WhatConverts doesn't care how a lead comes in—it tracks them all.
Dynamic Call Tracking:

It assigns unique phone numbers to your different marketing channels. When someone calls, you know exactly which ad, keyword, or page prompted the call. The call recording feature is gold for training sales teams and understanding customer intent.
Form Tracking:
It captures every form submission on your site and ties it directly to the marketing source. You can see the full form details right in your WhatConverts dashboard.
Chat Tracking:
Integrates with popular live chat tools to log conversations as leads, complete with their marketing source. No more lost chat leads in a separate system.
Detailed, Customizable Reporting

The default dashboard is great, but the real power is in the custom reports. You can build reports by source, medium, landing page, campaign, or even your own custom fields. Want to see the cost-per-lead from your LinkedIn ads versus your blog content? Easy. Need to report to a client or your boss? You can white-label and schedule reports to be sent automatically. It turns messy data into a clear story of what's driving business.
For pro tips on setting up your reports, we have a guide on best practices.
Lead Management & Qualification

WhatConverts helps you separate the tire-kickers from serious prospects. You can mark leads as "qualified" or "unqualified" directly in the system. This means you can report on *qualified* lead volume and cost, not just raw leads. You can also add notes, assign leads to team members, and set up alerts for high-value leads. It transforms your analytics from a passive reporting tool into an active part of your sales process.
New and Highlighted Features You Should Know About
WhatConverts isn't a static tool; it's constantly evolving based on user feedback. Here are some standout recent additions that show where the platform is headed.
Enhanced AI-Powered Insights

They've been rolling out more AI features to help you spot trends and anomalies faster. The system can now highlight significant changes in your lead flow, like a sudden spike in calls from a particular region or a drop in form submissions from a high-performing campaign. It's like having a junior analyst watching your data 24/7. This proactive intelligence saves hours of manual analysis.
Deeper Integrations & API

The integration library keeps growing, connecting seamlessly with CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot, call tracking solutions, and reporting tools like Google Data Studio. For businesses with custom workflows, their robust API allows you to push and pull data to build exactly what you need. This flexibility ensures WhatConverts fits into your stack, not the other way around.
Read about all the new features and updates in our dedicated article.
Improved Dashboard UX and Filtering

Recent updates have made the main dashboard even more intuitive and faster. Advanced filtering lets you drill down into your data with incredible precision—combine source, time frame, lead type, and custom tags to answer very specific questions. The user experience feels clean and focused, removing clutter so you can find what you need without a tutorial.
Who is WhatConverts Ideal For?
This tool is versatile, but it delivers exceptional value for specific types of users and businesses.
Digital Marketing Agencies:

This is a game-changer for client reporting. You can prove the exact ROI of your campaigns, justify ad spend, and demonstrate value with concrete lead data, not just impressions and clicks. The white-label reporting is a massive bonus.
Small to Medium Businesses (SMBs) with Sales Teams:

If you have a phone number or a contact form on your site, you need this. It helps SMBs maximize every marketing dollar by understanding what's generating real customer conversations. Sales managers love the call recordings and lead qualification features.
Enterprises Running Multi-Channel Campaigns:
For companies with complex marketing funnels across many channels, WhatConverts provides the unified, source-level attribution needed to understand true performance at scale.
See specific use cases for different industries in our deep dive.
Marketers Who Hate Wasting Budget:
If you've ever felt the frustration of not knowing which half of your advertising is working, WhatConverts is the answer. It's built for data-driven marketers who need to make informed decisions.
How WhatConverts Fits Into Your Marketing Stack
WhatConverts plays nicely with other tools. It's a central hub for lead data, not a replacement for your CRM or ad platform. Use it alongside your favorite tools to get the full picture.
With Your CRM:
Push qualified leads directly into Salesforce, HubSpot, or other CRMs so your sales team has all the context they need, including the original marketing source and call recording links.
With Your Ad Platforms:

Get the true conversion data from calls and forms back into Google Ads and Facebook Ads. This allows for smarter bidding strategies and audience optimization based on real leads, not just website conversions.
With Project Management & Ops Tools:
Use the data in WhatConverts to inform strategic decisions. For example, you might track leads by feature request to inform your product roadmap in tools like ClickUp or monday.com.
We have guides on integrating with those tools.
And this one for monday.com.
Getting Started with WhatConverts

The initial setup is designed to be simple. You add a small snippet of code to your website for form tracking, and then you set up your call tracking numbers—either porting existing numbers or acquiring new ones. The interface guides you through connecting your website and other sources. Within a few hours, data starts flowing in. Their help center is comprehensive, and their customer support is consistently praised in reviews (they have a 4.9/5 on G2 from over 300 reviews for a reason). The learning curve is gentle, and the payoff in clarity is almost immediate.
The Bottom Line: See What Really Converts

In a world full of vanity metrics, WhatConverts is a breath of fresh air. It gets you out of the weeds of session counts and bounce rates and puts you in control of understanding what actually generates business. The transparency it provides is empowering. You'll make better budget decisions, optimize your campaigns with precision, and finally have a direct line of sight between marketing activity and sales results.
It’s built for marketers and business owners who are serious about growth and demand proof of performance. If that's you, stop guessing and start knowing.
You can explore WhatConverts and start your free trial right here
to see exactly which campaigns are sending you real, trackable leads.

children.text: What is the main difference between WhatConverts and Google Analytics?

Google Analytics tracks website sessions and user behavior, but it struggles to connect offline conversions like phone calls back to specific marketing sources. WhatConverts is built specifically for lead attribution, tracking calls, forms, and chats as individual lead records and tying each one directly to its original marketing campaign, keyword, or ad. It provides lead-level detail and management, not just aggregate traffic data.
How does WhatConverts track phone calls?

It uses dynamic number insertion (DNI). When you add a WhatConverts script to your site, it swaps out the phone number displayed based on how the visitor arrived (e.g., from a Google Ad, a Facebook post, or organic search). Each source gets a unique number. When a call comes in on that number, the platform logs the marketing source, records the call (if enabled), and attributes it as a lead in your dashboard.
Is WhatConverts difficult to set up for a small business?

Not at all. The platform is designed for users of all technical levels. You install a simple JavaScript snippet on your website for form and chat tracking. For call tracking, you can either port your existing business phone number to their service or obtain new tracking numbers through their interface. Their onboarding guides and customer support are excellent, and most users are up and running with basic tracking in under an hour.
